campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that local companies pay to the city. Finance Department staff presented the proposed changes to these taxes, and the council discussed the matter publicly before voting on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases for fiscal year 2026
Finance department presentation on tax changes
Public council deliberation and vote on new rates
Municipal code updates to reflect revised tax amounts

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could voice opinions on a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by introducing a new ordinance and skipping the usual first-reading step to speed up the process, with plans to finalize it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite adoption
Ordinance scheduled for final approval at next regular council meeting
Community members had opportunity to speak before council decision

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the city manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services to support town improvements.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Adopted resolution for town improvements project

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 22, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 22, 2026 to organize and finalize their upcoming meeting agendas. The council reviewed what items would be discussed at their April 28 meeting, planned their May 5 meeting, and reviewed public records from the previous week.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 28, 2026 meeting agenda and added any last-minute items
Scheduled specific start times for certain agenda items if needed
Planned the May 5, 2026 draft agenda with potential new items
Reviewed public records from April 9-16, 2026
Checked notice requirements for any agenda items or documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a study session to review Mountain View's planned spending on infrastructure projects for the next fiscal year (2026-27), followed by a regular meeting where they officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week in the city.
keyTopics: 2026-27 budget for roads, buildings, and public facilities projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 21, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The City Council held a closed-door meeting at 6 P.M. to discuss confidential matters, followed by a regular public meeting at 7 P.M. where they saluted the flag and recognized some winners or achievement recipients. The specific details of what was decided in the closed session weren't publicly disclosed.
keyTopics: Closed session meeting (details not public)
Recognition of award winners or achievers
Regular council business and public meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 21, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss two ongoing legal cases that could expose the city to significant financial or legal risk, and to recognize the work of the Administrative Services Department's budget, finance, and purchasing teams. The meeting also included public comment time where residents could speak about any city matters not on the official agenda.
keyTopics: Two pending lawsuits with potential financial exposure to the city
Recognition of Administrative Services Department staff (Budget, Finance, and Purchasing divisions)
Open public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new three-year employment agreement with the city's firefighters union.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract covering 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting on April 21, 2026 to approve several financial and operational matters for the city. The council reviewed the city's tax and fiscal policies, approved the budget and development fees for the next fiscal year, discussed plans to address homelessness in Santa Clara County, and reviewed meeting minutes and upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Tax equity and fiscal responsibility policies reapproved
FY 2026-27 budget and development fees established
City fees and user charges updated
Santa Clara County homelessness prevention plan presented
Council meeting schedule and minutes reviewed

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council reviewed a request to reconsider a tree removal permit that had already been approved for a property at 751 Waverley Street. Someone is asking the Planning Director to take another look at the decision to allow protected trees to be removed from this location.
keyTopics: Protected tree removal permit at 751 Waverley Street
Request to have Planning Director reconsider an already-approved permit
Over-the-counter planning decision being challenged
